summary: Verified the onError fallback fix in AdminLayout.jsx & EmployeeLayout.jsx, and re-confirmed CheckInWidget non-regression on /admin. The seeded Acme Corp logo has been replaced with a valid 949x720 PNG (naturalWidth/Height read from live confirms this), so the primary happy path renders cleanly: sidebar 36x27 img inside 48x48 wrap, employee header 30x23 img inside 44x44 wrap — object-contain works, no overflow, no broken-image UI. Additionally, force-simulated an image error by pointing the sidebar .src at a 404 URL: the onError handler fires, logoBroken flips to true, React unmounts [data-testid='sidebar-company-logo'] and renders the Building2 fallback SVG inside the wrap (which shrinks to 40x40 per the else branch — h-10 w-10). Zero React errors, zero page errors. CheckInWidget still renders on /admin (DAY COMPLETE band as admin was left checked-out from iter7 — expected). CheckInWidget NOT rendered on /employee (correct — employee uses its own hero widget). All acceptance criteria satisfied.

issues: Pre-existing benign Recharts warning: 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' (appears twice on first render, does not affect functionality). Not a regression from this iteration.

critical_code_review_comments: AdminLayout.jsx & EmployeeLayout.jsx onError fix is clean and correct: showLogo = !!user?.company?.has_logo && !logoBroken. When onError fires, showLogo becomes false and React swaps to the fallback branch. Verified end-to-end.

Wrap element size changes between branches (48x48 with p-1.5 for image vs 40x40 solid accent for fallback). This is intentional (accent-colored fallback square matches the pre-existing has_logo=false design) but produces a subtle 8px layout shift when onError triggers post-mount. Minor / cosmetic — could unify to 48x48 if perfectly stable layout is required.

Backend seed logo has been fixed (now a 949x720 PNG). Recommend also adding image validation on POST /api/companies/{id}/logo (open with PIL, reject if fails) to prevent future bad uploads, and adding immutable cache-control headers for logo GETs — flagged in iter7 but still open.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 3 in-scope UI checks pass: fallback on-error, real-image render, checkin widget non-regression)

test_credentials: admin@acme.com/Admin@123 (super_admin), maya@acme.com/Demo@123 (employee) — both verified this iteration
seed_data_creation: None. admin@acme.com left in DAY COMPLETE (checked-out) state at end of test as required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Layout onError fallback + logo rendering + CheckInWidget non-regression are all GREEN. Backend seed logo is now a real 949x720 PNG (was 10 bytes ASCII in iter7). If future iterations touch layouts or the /companies/{id}/logo endpoint, re-run: (a) load /admin as admin@acme.com and evaluate document.querySelector('[data-testid="sidebar-company-logo"]').naturalWidth > 0, (b) simulate onError by setting img.src to a bogus /logo URL and confirm the element is unmounted + [data-testid='sidebar-logo-wrap'] > svg exists, (c) same for [data-testid='emp-company-logo']. CheckInWidget backend tests already 100% green — trust them.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration was a fix verification, not a bug hunt. Iter7's RCA (10-byte ASCII stub served as image/png → broken-image UI + alt overflow) is now fully mitigated on TWO layers: (1) backend seed replaced with a real 949x720 PNG (primary fix, verified via naturalWidth), and (2) frontend onError handler in both layout components swaps to the Building2/Briefcase fallback icon whenever any future image load fails (defensive fix, verified by simulating a 404 URL and observing React unmount the + render the fallback SVG).
